content:
A single sentence in your arbitration clause can decide whether your dispute is resolved in months or gets stuck in litigation for years.

Most commercial disputes become expensive not because the case is weak, but because the arbitration clause is poorly drafted. Missing the seat of arbitration, using uncertain language, conflicting jurisdiction clauses, or creating an unworkable appointment mechanism can force parties into court even before arbitration begins.

In this carousel, you'll learn: ✔️ How to draft a legally enforceable arbitration clause. ✔️ The essential elements every clause must contain. ✔️ The five most common drafting mistakes. ✔️ A ready-to-use model arbitration clause. ✔️ A practical checklist to review before signing any commercial contract.

Whether you're a lawyer, entrepreneur, in-house counsel, or law student, mastering arbitration clause drafting can save years of unnecessary litigation.

💬 Which mistake do you see most often in commercial agreements? Let me know in the comments.

📌 Save this post for your next contract review. 🔄 Share it with someone who drafts commercial agreements.
